summary: Large game-mode decompilation PR for src/melee/gm/gm_19EF.c, replacing placeholder comments with C for Game Over / Continue-style scene setup and update logic. It added full matches for fn_8019F810, fn_8019F6EC, gm_801A0A10_OnEnter, fn_8019F1D0, and .bss, while bringing fn_8019F9C4, fn_8019F2D4, and fn_8019EFC4 close to matching. The PR also refined prototypes for fn_8019F9C4 and fn_80168F2C so callers could pass the discovered arguments.
what_changed: Expanded gm_19EF.c from mostly placeholders to 668 lines of C with static globals, a reconstructed local state struct lbl_80479A98, scene archive pointers, JObj/MObj handles, and update helpers.

Implemented static helper fn_8019EFC4 for coin/countdown JObj animation, including digit-frame math, visibility flag toggling with HSD_JObjSetFlags/HSD_JObjClearFlags, and child/next traversal of the loaded model hierarchy.
Implemented fn_8019F1D0 to convert lbl_80479A98.x8 to decimal text manually and render it through HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 at 240.0f, 176.0f.
Implemented fn_8019F2D4 as the main input/state transition helper, using controller trigger masks, state values 0, 1, 8, 9, 0xA, JObj/MObj animation requests, and lbAudioAx calls.
Implemented fn_8019F6EC and fn_8019F810 as GObj procs for per-frame animation updates, score countdown/transition handling, and eventual gm_801A4B60 scene progression.

Implemented fn_8019F9C4(u32 arg0), a large scene construction routine that loads GmGover.dat, GmGoCoin.dat, GmGoAnim.dat, GmRgStnd.dat, character-specific model symbols, cameras, lights, JObjs, SisLib text, and audio cues.

Implemented gm_801A0A10_OnEnter(void*) to unpack DebugGameOverData into lbl_80479A98 fields, clamp a derived value to 0x3E7, compute a decrement step over 120 frames, store player slot/character kind, and call fn_8019F9C4.
Changed gm_19EF.h declaration for fn_8019F9C4 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_8019F9C4(u32).
Changed gm_1601.c and gm_1601.h prototype for fn_80168F2C from void(void) to void(s8), with the implementation naming the parameter ignored; gm_19EF now calls fn_80168F2C(0).
Added includes needed for the new matched code, including gm_1601.h, gm_1A3F.h, gm_1A45.h, it/inlines.h, controller.h, dobj.h, mobj.h, lb_00B0.h, toy.h, tylist.h, and sislib.h.
smart_moves: Kept still-unknown global and field names address/offset based instead of inventing semantics; lbl_80479A98 uses x0/x4/x8-style fields with explicit pad_XX gaps, which preserves layout evidence for later renaming.
Updated cross-file prototypes only when the new call evidence required it, especially fn_80168F2C(s8 ignored) and fn_8019F9C4(u32), reducing UNK_PARAMS usage while acknowledging unused parameters.
Used existing baselib types and APIs instead of opaque pointers: HSD_Archive, SceneDesc, HSD_Joint, HSD_JObj, HSD_MObj, HSD_GObj, HSD_Text, and HSD_PadStatus.
Matched model hierarchy traversal explicitly with repeated NULL checks for child and next pointers, a common decomp tactic when generated code needs the same branch structure as the original.
Separated scene setup, per-frame GObj proc logic, input/state transitions, text drawing, and small animation updates into distinct functions matching the original function boundaries.
Preserved matching-sensitive casts such as (u8), (u16), (s8), and (f32), and included PAD_STACK annotations where needed.
Documented an unresolved type/width issue in-place with the gx_link assignment: cam_gobj->gx_link = (u8) 0x801 with a TODO noting that 0x801 does not fit in u8.
decomp_lessons: For game-mode scene setup functions, matching often depends on reconstructing the exact static state layout before attempting the large loader function.
Do not leave cross-module helper prototypes as void(void) or UNK_PARAMS once callsites reveal an argument; even an ignored s8 parameter can be necessary for correct call ABI and matching.

Scene decomp benefits from following asset names and symbol strings: GmGover.dat, GmGoCoin.dat, GmGoAnim.dat, GmRgStnd.dat, ScGamRegGover_scene_data, standScene, SdIntro.dat, and SIS_IntroData all anchor the function's purpose without guessing.
Manual integer-to-string conversion can be matching-sensitive; replacing it with sprintf-like helpers would likely not match this codegen.
Repeated HSD_JObjReqAnimAll followed by HSD_JObjAnimAll and HSD_MObjReqAnim/HSD_MObjAnim sequences should be preserved in order for animation-state functions.
When the model tree traversal is repetitive, resist over-abstracting; explicit NULL-guarded child/next chains may be required for matching.
Use address-based names for file-local functions such as fn_8019F1D0, fn_8019F2D4, fn_8019F6EC, fn_8019F810, and fn_8019F9C4 until semantic names are proven.
naming_conventions: Unidentified file-local globals retained lbl_804D66xx/lbl_80479A98 labels.
Anonymous reconstructed structs used x0/x4 fields and pad_XX names to indicate known offsets without overclaiming semantics.
Unknown or not-yet-renamed functions retained fn_8019.... address names.
Known lifecycle function kept subsystem-style name gm_801A0A10_OnEnter.
Newly understood prototype in gm_19EF.h uses concrete void return and u32 argument instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Unused discovered parameter in fn_80168F2C was named ignored, making the ABI change explicit without suggesting behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(16) in fn_8019F810 and PAD_STACK(8) in fn_8019F9C4.
Used explicit casts to u8, u16, s8, u32, f32, and function-pointer casts for HSD_GObj_SetupProc where needed.
Preserved branchy NULL-check patterns for JObj child/next traversal instead of simplifying to helper expressions.
Kept modulo/division arithmetic for digit animation frames: ones/tens/hundreds values mapped through multiples of 5 modulo 50.
Used ternary expression for clamping score decrement in fn_8019F810.
Matched controller input using raw trigger bit masks such as 0x1100, 0x40000, and 0x80000.
Mode-specific switch over gm_801A4310 selected MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_ADVENTURE, or MJ_ALLSTAR paths for gm_80160474.
The decomp.dev bot reported full matches for fn_8019F810, fn_8019F6EC, gm_801A0A10_OnEnter, fn_8019F1D0, and .bss, and near matches for fn_8019F9C4, fn_8019F2D4, and fn_8019EFC4.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
PR body only said: Tracking PR as usual.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code improved by +0.03% / +1252 bytes and matched data improved by +0.01% / +120 bytes.
Bot reported 5 new matches: fn_8019F810 (+436), fn_8019F6EC (+292), gm_801A0A10_OnEnter (+264), fn_8019F1D0 (+260), and gm_19EF .bss (+60).
Bot reported 6 unmatched-item improvements: fn_8019F9C4 to 97.19%, fn_8019F2D4 to 99.48%, fn_8019EFC4 to 98.38%, .data to 86.07%, .sbss to 84.62%, and .sdata2 to 91.67%.
Bot reported one data regression: gm_19EF .sdata decreased by 2 bytes, from 56.52% to 51.52%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_80479A98 fields or DebugGameOverData fields used by gm_801A0A10_OnEnter.
Search for a later fix to cam_gobj->gx_link type width, especially the TODO about assigning 0x801 to a u8.
Look for subsequent PRs completing fn_8019F9C4, fn_8019F2D4, and fn_8019EFC4 from near-match to full match.
Check whether fn_80168F2C(s8 ignored) later receives a semantic name or parameter meaning.
Search other gm_19EF or game-over PRs for asset names GmGover.dat, GmGoCoin.dat, GmGoAnim.dat, and GmRgStnd.dat.
